content.fact: By Chapter 431, Lemillion is identified as the current Number One Hero.
content.injection: In More-era ranking scenes, Lemillion can carry current Number One Hero status; do not give that position back to All Might, Endeavor, or another earlier-era symbol.

entries.title: Former Class A Gather As Working Adults

content.fact: Former Class A members gather as adults with careers, agencies, sidekick roles, rankings, teaching, and public responsibilities.
content.injection: Write the Chapter 431 reunion as adults with separate lives reconnecting, not as students who see each other every day at school.

entries.title: Shoto's Number Two Ranking Is The Dinner Context

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia Chapter 431 'More' from Volume 42. Optional post-series extra overlay after Chapter 430; not loaded by default for the Chapter 430 epilogue deck.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded unless added as separate overlay decks. Chapter 431 material should not leak into earlier school-era, war, or Chapter 430 scenes unless this overlay is active.

content.fact: The former classmates gather in part to celebrate Shoto Todoroki's Number Two ranking on the Hero Billboard Chart JP.
content.injection: Use Shoto's high ranking as the reunion's social catalyst and as proof of his adult hero standing, while keeping the mood reflective rather than purely triumphal.

entries.title: Shoto Seeks A Life Beyond Hero Destiny

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia Chapter 431 'More' from Volume 42. Optional post-series extra overlay after Chapter 430; not loaded by default for the Chapter 430 epilogue deck.

content.fact: Chapter 431 frames Shoto's adult life as more than hero destiny, including reflection on Toya, food, craft, and ordinary personal wants.
content.injection: In More-era Shoto scenes, let him be a top hero who is also trying to discover ordinary pleasures and personal choices beyond the Todoroki family tragedy.

entries.title: Toya Is Dead By The More Era

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia Chapter 431 'More' from Volume 42. Optional post-series extra overlay after Chapter 430; not loaded by default for the Chapter 430 epilogue deck.

content.fact: Chapter 431 indicates Toya Todoroki died sometime during the eight-year postwar interval.
content.injection: In More-era canon, Toya/Dabi should be treated as dead and memorialized, not alive in custody or available for ordinary reunion scenes unless the story intentionally diverges.

entries.title: Deku Remains A Teacher By Choice

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia Chapter 431 'More' from Volume 42. Optional post-series extra overlay after Chapter 430; not loaded by default for the Chapter 430 epilogue deck.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded unless added as separate overlay decks. Chapter 431 material should not leak into earlier school-era, war, or Chapter 430 scenes unless this overlay is active.

content.fact: Deku states that teaching remains meaningful to him and that the support suit lets him do both work he loves and hero activity.
content.injection: In More-era scenes, Deku must not be written as resentful that teaching replaced hero work. He values teaching and uses the suit to keep hero activity possible.

content.fact: Bakugo's interest in Deku's work and agency possibilities is expressed indirectly through teasing, pressure, and abrasive concern rather than open softness.
content.injection: Write More-era Bakugo as matured but still difficult: his concern can appear through blunt criticism, rivalry, and indirect offers rather than easy sentimentality.

entries.title: Adult Hero Rankings Are Broader Than Old Symbol Order

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia Chapter 431 'More' from Volume 42. Optional post-series extra overlay after Chapter 430; not loaded by default for the Chapter 430 epilogue deck.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded unless added as separate overlay decks. Chapter 431 material should not leak into earlier school-era, war, or Chapter 430 scenes unless this overlay is active.

content.fact: Chapter 431 presents a rebuilt hero ranking environment with multiple adult peers rather than a simple return to the All Might/Endeavor-era symbol hierarchy.
content.injection: Treat postwar rankings as a broader professional ecosystem. Public rank can matter, but the old single-symbol model must not be restored wholesale.

entries.title: A Sudden Incident Shows Heroes Are Still Needed

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia Chapter 431 'More' from Volume 42. Optional post-series extra overlay after Chapter 430; not loaded by default for the Chapter 430 epilogue deck.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded unless added as separate overlay decks. Chapter 431 material should not leak into earlier school-era, war, or Chapter 430 scenes unless this overlay is active.

content.fact: A criminal incident interrupts the reunion and is quickly handled, showing that hero work still exists even as villain numbers decline.
content.injection: In More-era public-safety scenes, heroes remain needed, but incidents can be smaller, faster-contained, and rooted in sudden impulse rather than organized war.

entries.title: Villains Are Decreasing But Not Gone

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia Chapter 431 'More' from Volume 42. Optional post-series extra overlay after Chapter 430; not loaded by default for the Chapter 430 epilogue deck.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded unless added as separate overlay decks. Chapter 431 material should not leak into earlier school-era, war, or Chapter 430 scenes unless this overlay is active.

content.fact: The former classmates discuss a future where villain numbers are decreasing, but hero work has not disappeared.
content.injection: Use More-era society as safer but not utopian: villain activity may decline year by year, yet heroes and support systems are still necessary.
